specification.topic-statement: The combined terminal renderer assembles a complete terminal framebuffer from scrollback content, a separator line, and the input buffer, writing the result in a single atomic operation.

scope.in-scope: Building the complete ANSI framebuffer from scrollback lines, separator, and input buffer text; cursor screen position calculation from a byte offset; and the single-field input buffer renderer used in testing contexts.
scope.boundary: The combined renderer is the legacy path used when the layer cake is absent. In production, the layer cake renders each component separately. This topic covers the combined renderer only.
The scrollback provides pre-computed line layouts. This topic requests layout synchronization and reads physical line counts and text.
The framebuffer is written to the terminal file descriptor in a single write syscall. Terminal management is handled externally.

behavior.cursor-screen-position-calculation:
Given text content and a cursor byte offset, the screen row and column of the cursor are calculated by iterating through the text from position zero to the byte offset, tracking row and column:
- Each Unicode codepoint is decoded from UTF-8 to determine its display width. Wide characters (e.g., CJK) count as 2 columns; combining characters count as 0. - ANSI CSI escape sequences (beginning with ESC followed by '[') are skipped entirely: all parameter bytes (0x30–0x3F) and intermediate bytes (0x20–0x2F) are consumed without affecting column; the final byte (0x40–0x7E) ends the sequence. - When a newline character is encountered, the row increments and the column resets to zero. - When adding a character's display width would cause the column to equal or exceed the terminal width, the row increments and the column resets to zero before placing the character.
The result is a zero-based screen row and zero-based screen column.
behavior.input-buffer-render:
A framebuffer is built containing: 1. Clear entire screen (ANSI ED2) 2. Hide cursor 3. Home cursor (top-left) 4. Input text with all newlines converted to CRLF 5. Cursor positioning at the computed screen coordinates (1-based row and column)
The complete framebuffer is written in one atomic syscall.
behavior.combined-render:
The scrollback layout is synchronized to the current terminal width before rendering. A framebuffer is assembled containing:
Clear entire screen, hide cursor, home cursor.
Scrollback lines are rendered in order from scrollback_start_line for scrollback_line_count logical lines. Each logical line's text is copied with newlines converted to CRLF. A CRLF is added after each line except potentially the last:
If the last scrollback line is the final element to render (no separator and no input buffer follow), the trailing CRLF is omitted. This prevents the terminal from auto-scrolling when the last line is at the bottom of the screen.
If the separator is visible, a row of '-' characters (terminal width number of dashes) is written. A CRLF follows the separator unless the input buffer does not follow.
If the input buffer is visible, its text is written with newlines converted to CRLF. No trailing CRLF is added.
The cursor is shown (ANSI DECTCEM) if the input buffer is visible, hidden otherwise. If visible, the cursor is positioned at: - row = sum of physical lines used by rendered scrollback lines + input cursor's screen row within the input buffer - column = input cursor's screen column
Coordinates are 1-based in the ANSI positioning escape sequence.
The complete framebuffer is written in one atomic syscall. A write error is returned unless the tty file descriptor is -1 (headless mode).
